headline: New AES owners could impact your electric bill -- eventually, consumers' office says
summary: Apr. 17—Ohio's consumer watchdog office says the likely purchase of AES Ohio's parent company is not expected to result in immediate rate increases, but rate increases are possible down the road. The Office of the Ohio Consumers' Counsel will watch how the acquisition unfolds, said Angela O'Brien the deputy consumers' counsel for the office. "OCC always has a general concern about ...
